title: nothoscordum hirtellum subsp. lorentzii
intro: Nothoscordum hirtellum subsp. lorentzii, also known as the Patagonian onion or wild garlic, is a plant species belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Nothoscordum hirtellum subsp. lorentzii include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves can be consumed raw or used in salads.
